body_txt: A character is subjected to something which is clearly, obviously, incontrovertibly, and usually quite messily fatal, or at the very least extremely painful. There's a moment's pause for comic effect ... And from the wreckage emerges a muffled "I'm OK.", sometimes accompanied by a thumbs-up. This trope is always Played for Laughs — if there is the least bit of ambiguity if something is Slapstick or dramatic, this trope serves to break the tension and yield to the Rule of Funny . Compare/Contrast He's Okay , in which another character notes that the injured party isn't dead and in a usually serious situation. In works where Nobody Can Die , this can be an Enforced Element — and also (as an added bonus) a comical means of Getting Crap Past the Radar . A character getting seriously hurt or killed by violence would be Family-Unfriendly and therefore unacceptable... but it they suffer the exact same violence and verbally declare they are okay? That's fine! Don't think about how they could be unaffected, just Take Our Word for It ! This way, a work can display an event so violent No One Could Survive That! ...then quickly remind you that of course they did — what kind of show do you think you're watching? Compare Could Have Been Messy . See also Non Sequitur, *Thud* for another common outcome of this scenario. Not to be confused with I'm O.K , a video game. Compare and contrast Obviously Not Fine where they say the line, but everyone can see it's not true. Here is a compilation with a lot of examples.
